world_name: Legend of the Five Rings - Rokugan
prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG Japanese feudal fantasy atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Legend of the Five Rings / Rokugan inspired Japanese feudal fantasy map, diverse archipelago terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye atlas view of a vast misty sea filled with many samurai-era island groups, microcontinents, and elemental spirit routes.
Subject: diverse archipelago layout with uneven island distribution across a broad misty ocean: several large province-islands, microcontinents, tiny shrine islets, curved island arcs, lagoons, reef belts, narrow straits, remote atolls, craggy islands, fragmented rocky coasts, castle islands, sacred forest islands, volcanic islands, rice-plain islands, cherry blossom islands, tundra highlands, wetland islands, Shadowlands-tainted forbidden islands, and spirit-realm islands.
Style/medium: Rokugan map, Japanese feudal empire provinces, elemental rings aesthetic, mon crest-like non-text symbols, ink-and-watercolor atlas, highly detailed illustrated RPG sourcebook cartography.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, no single dominant mainland, island groups sweep in organic arcs and clusters, full archipelago visible with misty sea border.
Lighting/mood: honorable samurai epic, cherry blossom atmosphere, honor and duty, clan politics, court intrigue, elemental spirits, hidden naval routes and sacred islands.
Color palette: washi parchment, sumi ink black, muted jade green, cherry blossom pink, imperial red, gold accents, mist blue sea, shadowlands sickly grey-green.
Materials/textures: washi paper, brush ink coastlines, watercolor washes, delicate mon-like symbols without readable characters, detailed waves and shoals.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable Japanese or Chinese characters, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Avoid grid-like spacing and avoid identical island shapes.
